parameters.subject: =Welcome to Caddy Build Phase (Part I), Team {{ $('Get row(s)').item.json.client_name }}!
parameters.message:
= Hello, World!

Hi Team {{ $('Get row(s)').item.json.client_name }},

Welcome to the Build Phase! We're excited to move forward with you.

Please start by watching the Presets Upload Tutorial (Part I) for detailed instructions and next steps.

Here is your dedicated Presets Upload Template. Once you have finished Part I of the Presets Template, our team will review to make sure everything looks good before moving on to Part II.

If you have any questions, feel free to reach out to us and we would be happy to help. 

Best regards,

Ron

parameters.subject: Caddy Knowledge Base [BOOKMARK ME]
parameters.message:
= Training Phase Instructions
Hi Team {{ $json.client_name }},

Great job mapping all your batch codes to their respective batch formulas!

Here is the link to the Caddy Knowledge Base.

This is the home to our (growing) library of video tutorials for not just the Training Phase of our implementation process, but should you ever need to reference back on any tutorial moving forward (great to share with new team members as well!).

Can't find a tutorial you're looking for? Send me a note and we'll be happy to either point you to the right page or record a video tutorial for you.

Please have all Caddy users watch all the video tutorials and flag any scenarios where a video tutorial would be helpful. Once this task is completed, kindly update your Implementation Checklist by marking an "X" in Column F.

After the Training Phase, we can move onto the Testing Phase next week where you will simulate daily workflows in your environment before we go-live.

Gabrielle, please start preparing their inventory upload template.

Pro tip: bookmark the Caddy Knowledge Base for easy access.

Ron

--
Ron Leung
Chief Executive Officer, Caddy
C: +1.416.305.6148
See Caddy in Action



Core Support Hours: Mon-Fri, 9:00 AM ET to 6:00 PM ET (Responses may be delayed outside these hours)

parameters.subject: Caddy | Time to activate your accounts
parameters.message:
= Welcome to Caddy

Hi Team {{ $('Get row(s)').item.json.client_name }},

Your Presets have been uploaded.

You should have also just received an email invite to activate your license. There's a 12 hour window before the invite expires. If it does expire, please let us know so we can re-send the invite.

To fully activate your license(s), you'll need to sign into your mobile app with your unique 4 digit pin.

Once you're in, the next step is to go into your Presets tab, inside your Admin Portal, to set up your Batch Codes (lot code templates for your batch formulas) for each Batch Formula. Once you've completed each batch code template, please map them to each batch formula, also located inside your Presets tab.

Tip: to future proof your business and limit the scope of potential future recalls, please consider adding unique identifiers to each batch formula's batch code template.

Once you've completed this task, please let us know and we'll review it before moving into the training phase.

Thanks!
Ron

--

Ron Leung
Chief Executive Officer, Caddy
C: +1.416.305.6148
See Caddy in Action



Core Support Hours: Mon-Fri, 9:00 AM ET to 6:00 PM ET (Responses may be delayed outside these hours)

parameters.subject: =Welcome to Caddy Test Phase, Team {{ $('Get row(s)').item.json.client_name }}!
parameters.message:
= Hello, World!

Hi Team {{ $('Get row(s)').item.json.client_name }},

Welcome to the Test Phase! We're excited to move forward with you.

Please start by watching the Caddy Test Phase Overview video for detailed instructions and next steps.

Carlo will help populate your admin portal with sample data including batches, packaging, and associated raw materials. Once this is ready, Carlo will notify via this email thread. This will give your team a solid foundation to start your testing.

You can manually add additional raw or packaging materials through the admin portal or the mobile app, which is one of your test scenarios. For products beyond the ones Carlo will set up, you'll need to submit production and packaging forms via the mobile app to create batches and finished products.

Here is your dedicated Test Phase Bingo Card . As you complete each task or scenario, please check off the corresponding boxes.

If you encounter critical blockers that may prevent a successful go-live, please note them in the bottom section of the Bingo Card. Reserve this space for "must-have" issues only. Any "nice-to-have" feedback can be discussed and addressed after we go live.

This approach helps us stay focused and ensures we get you up and running quickly and smoothly.

Best regards,

Ron

--
Ron Leung
Chief Executive Officer, Caddy
C: +1.416.305.6148
See Caddy in Action

parameters.subject: =Welcome to Caddy Launch Phase, Team {{ $('Get row(s)').item.json.client_name }}!
parameters.message:
= Hello, World!
Hi Team {{ $('Get row(s)').item.json.client_name }},

We're in the final stretch!

Here are the 3 action items (to be completed in sequence) for next steps:
  1. Watch the Inventory Upload Tutorial
  2. Complete your Inventory Upload Template (Note: please include all existing lot/product #'s associated with your existing inventory on-hand)
  3. Notify us once completed so our team can review and then upload into your environment
Once all 3 action items are completed, we will have officially gone live. 

We're almost there, keep up the great work!

Ron

--
Ron Leung
Chief Executive Officer, Caddy

= Hello, World!
Congratulations Team {{ $('Get row(s)').item.json.client_name }}! 

We did it.

The inventory count has been uploaded so we're now officially up and running.

This was all hands on deck and I deeply appreciate the effort everybody put into achieving this milestone.

Gabrielle will help schedule our first quarterly check-in.

Gabrielle, {{ $('Get row(s)').item.json.client_name }} is based in {{ ($("HTTP Request7")?.first()?.json?.properties?.hs_timezone?.value ?? "").split("_slash_").map(p => p.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + p.slice(1)).join("/"); }}.

Excited to build and grow together!

Ron
